patch_description:
Proposes a parameter-efficient ANSATZ_SPEC revision for the 9-qubit tic-tac-toe QML task. 
- Ring entanglement uses a single shared CRX parameter around the outer perimeter (crx_ring).
- Hub entanglement uses two shared parameters across opposite edges: hub_crx for (center, 1) and (center, 5); hub_cry for (center, 3) and (center, 7). This enforces symmetry and reduces parameter count.
- All other layers preserve the fixed pre/post RZ and RY symmetry-based sharing, ensuring compatibility with the training loop.
- Total parameters per block drop from 13 to 12 (72 total after accounting for 3 uploads and 2 repeats), which should improve generalization and stabilize training while maintaining expressivity.
- Gates remain on allowed adjacent grid edges; the design remains compatible with the existing training pipeline (Adam, etc.).

This approach aims to improve validation/test performance and reduce overfitting by lowering parameter count and promoting symmetric, bipartisan hub-ring interactions.
